name: Rampart
description:
Rampart is a strategy game centered around claiming territory by building walls and cannons. The game is played on a tile-based map where players compete to control castles. The gameplay is divided into distinct phases: a building phase, a deployment phase, and an attack phase. 

During the building phase, players strategically place walls to enclose territory. The goal is to create a closed perimeter containing at least one castle. Completing an enclosure claims the territory within. Players are limited to a set number of wall segments per building phase. Walls must connect to existing structures (walls or castles). If an enclosure is not completed when the building phase ends, the player loses any incomplete sections. 

In the deployment phase, the player populates the enclosed territory by placing cannons within them. Cannons provide defense during the attack phase. The number of cannons available depends on the size of the territory claimed and can be influenced by specific strategic advantages.

During the attack phase, players fire cannons at each other's walls and castles. The player chooses a cannon and a target (wall or castle) within range, and the cannon fires. Destroying all of a player's castles eliminates that player. Walls take a single hit to destroy, and castles require multiple hits. After each round of firing, if one player still has castles, the building, deployment and attack phases will start again. 

The game continues until only one player remains or no players are left with castles. The game may also result in a draw.

controls.z: Place building piece / Fire selected cannon
controls.x: Cancel building piece placement / Cancel cannon fire
controls.c: End turn/End Building Phase/End Deployment Phase
